Adapter will be stoped: The LoRaWAN Bridge Adapter will be stoped
AuthenticationInformation: Set the authentication for your server (if needed)
BridgeConnectionTestTooltip: cklick this button, to check to connection to your bridge
BridgeDevicesToGenerateTooltip: select the desired devicetypes, you like to see in device manager
BridgeEnumTooltip: Select an enum, that is used for bringing foreign states to the bridge
BridgeInformation: Main configuration for bridge via mqtt discovery
BridgeStateInformation: Here you can define an array of states, that will be set a Topic for Discovery. (Wildcards are possible too)
BridgeTypeTooltip: Select the Type of the foreign System
ClimateForeignInformation: Here you can define an array of Climate definitions
ClimateInformation: Here you can define an array of Climate definitions
CoverClosedSignalTooltip: Choose a Boolean signal that indicates the closed state
CoverClosedSignalTrueTooltip: select true, if signal brings value true in cas of cover is closed
CoverHeader: Define States for a Cover Entity
CoverInformation: Here you can define an array of Cover definitions
CoverNumberSignalTooltip: Choose, if you have a number signal with the signals for limit switch (OPENING, OPENED, CLOSING, CLSOED)
CoverOpenSignalTooltip: Choose a Boolean signal that indicates the open state
CoverOpenSignalTrueTooltip: select true, if signal brings value true in cas of cover is open
CoverSeparateTooltip: selected: separate boolean states for open / close. Otherwise, one string state.
CoverSimulatePositionTooltip: simulates the position depending on the limit switches (required for some systems)
CoverStopTooltip: An additional state for stop command
EnableRefreshTooltip: enable to discover in a cyclic cron
HumidifierHeader: Define States for a Humidifier Entity
HumidifierInformation: Here you can define an array of Humidifier definitions
LightColorTemperatureTooltip: enable to set color temperature id
LightHeader: Define States for a Light Entity
LightInformation: Here you can define an array of Light definitions
LoRaWAN device is back online: The following LoRaWAN device is back online
LoRaWAN device is offline: The following LoRaWAN device is offline
LockHeader: Define States for a Lock Entity
LockInformation: Here you can define an array of Lock definitions
LockOpenTooltip: selected: separate boolean states for open
LockSeparateStateTooltip: selected: separate state for the state of the lock
LockSeparateTooltip: selected: separate boolean states for lock, unlock. Otherwise, one string state.
MowerHeader: Define States for a Lawn-Mower Entity
MowerInformation: Here you can define an array of Lawn-Mower definitions
OriginInformation: Set the origin of the expected data
RefreshDiscoveryCronJobTooltip: insert the cronjob for refreshing the discovery
Serverinformation: Set the main communication settings to your server
Set the Base Ip for LoRaWAN devices: Set the Base Ip for LoRaWAN devices
Set the Base Ip for bride devices: Set the Base Ip for bride devices
StatciTextMowerSeparate: The default values are selected for the worx adapter
VirtualModeTooltip: Activate, if a mode id is not present
WithActTooltip: activate for setting id for actual value
_BridgeEnumDiscription:
Select a Enum tha is used for setting states into Bridge.
The name of the device is following this priorities:
1. Devicename
2. Channelname
3. Namespace of state
_DeviceIdentifierDiscription: Configure the identifiers to create an MQTT topic for a state. You can select multiple device identifiers, separated by the separator.
_NoLoRaWAN: The State is not in the right LoRaWAN instance
commandForOpenTooltip: set the command, that opens the lock
confirmedTooltip: if you like to send the download with confirmed message enable this option
connection to bridge is closed: connection to bridge will be closed
crcTooltip: append the crc16 to your data
customConfigHeader: Add a specific custom Object to each decoded state
customConfigurationJsonActiveTooltip: With this function you add the desired JSON on the custom entrie from the configuration channel
customControlJsonActiveTooltip: With this function you add the desired JSON on the custom entrie from the control channel
customDeleteDesciption: To delete an entry, please enter the entry with content "null".
customDeleteDescriptionImageTooltip: Information about removing an existing entry.
customDesciption: The folwing picture shows you an example for data in the JSON-EDITOR. For more informations use the documentation.
customDescriptionImageTooltip: Please fill the Editor with desired json you like to have in common.custom object of the decoded states.
customJsonActiveTooltip: With this function it is possible to add your own custom entries to the decoded states
customJsonInformation: With this function it is possible to add your own custom entries to the decoded states. Just input the desired json content in active function.
customJsonTooltip: Add a new Object, wich will be written into common.custom.
decimalPlacesTooltip: select the desired numberof decimal places
deviceTypeTooltip: insert the name of the device type, this config is valid. (eg. Dragino XY also matches Dragino)
endTooltip: insert the end-value, wich will be set to the downlink (value must be in hex - exept type string)
frontTooltip: insert the front-value, wich will be set to the downlink (value must be in hex - exept type string)
ipUrl: url or ip of the server
ipUrlTooltip: input the ip-address, or the url you want to connect to
keepaliveTooltip: input the time in seconds for keepalive function
lengthInByteTooltip: insert the length of the state in bytes
limitMaxTooltip: limits the possibility of inputs in positive direction
limitMaxValueTooltip: input the limit in positive direction
limitMinTooltip: limits the possibility of inputs in negative direction
limitMinValueTooltip: input the limit in negative direction
multiplyfaktorTooltip: insert the faktor you want to multiply your data (eg. 60 for input 1(min) => 60s)
name: name
nameTooltip: insert the name of the state, for this dowlink
offTooltip: insert the value you want to send if you set the state to false
onClickTooltip: insert the value you want to send if you set the state to true (button click)
onTooltip: insert the value you want to send if you set the state to true
originTooltip: select the origin of your data
portDownlinkTooltip: input the port you want to send in the downlink
portTooltip: input the port for the connection
priorityTooltip: input the priority you want to send in the downlink
sendWithUplinkTooltip: choose your send mode for configs with the desired devicetype
separatorTooltip: choose a separator for the identifiers
sslTooltip: if your connection uses ssl, please enable this checkbox (hppts instead http is used)
swapTooltip: swap the byteoder of the input
typeTooltip: select the type of input you like to chose
unitTooltip: insert the unit for your value
MowingStateNumberTooltip: Number to indicate the mowing state
PauseStateNumberTooltip: Number to indicate the pause state
HomeStateNumberTooltip: Number to indicate the home state
StartNumberTooltip: Enter ther Number wich will be send to start
StopNumberTooltip: Enter ther Number wich will be send to stop
HomeNumberTooltip: Enter ther Number wich will be send to home
statesValueTooltip: Enter a JSON for the states of this parameter
